description: Student-facing quiz integration: EnhancedQuizOverlay and ProgressGateModal wired into lesson player page, ZodError .issues verified in 4 API files, fill_in_blank enum verified, dead code removed.

Create comprehensive MVP documentation package: Privacy Policy (GDPR-compliant), Terms of Service (educational platform appropriate), AI Safety & Moderation Guide, Deployment Guide, and updated README. Critical for NGO deployment and legal compliance.
reviewNotes: Excellent documentation package. 1,781 lines across 5 files. All 8 acceptance criteria met. (1) Privacy Policy (290 lines): Comprehensive GDPR/COPPA/UNCRC coverage — data types, legal basis, retention table, children's protections, third-party processors (Supabase, OpenAI), international transfers, security measures, user rights. Professional NGO-ready quality. (2) Terms of Service (327 lines): Appropriate for educational platform with children — acceptable use, AI tutor limitations, homework integrity, child safety, liability disclaimers. (3) AI Safety & Moderation Guide (383 lines): Outstanding — references actual codebase (SYSTEM_PROMPT, checkRateLimit, tool names all verified against route.ts). 4-level escalation framework, trauma-informed design principles, tool usage table with guardrails, audit schedule. Honest about what's implemented vs. planned (Moderation API noted as recommended addition). (4) Deployment Guide (474 lines): 9-step actionable guide — Supabase setup, OpenAI config, env vars, Vercel deployment, post-deployment verification (health/performance/security/compliance), monitoring, troubleshooting, rollback, scaling considerations with cost estimates. (5) README (307 lines): Mission-driven, feature breakdown by role, tech stack, getting started guide, project structure, contributing guidelines, and roadmap. Minor: placeholder values for jurisdiction, GitHub URL, and contact emails — expected for MVP docs, appropriately bracketed for human completion. Build passes cleanly (0 TS errors). Clean conventional commit.

task: Fix Final TypeScript Errors in Guardian Portal (Opus Build)
description: Fix 2 remaining TS errors blocking MS-004 build: (1) relationship_type type assertion in guardian/dashboard, (2) homework_assignments!inner query type inference in guardian/students/[id]. Escalated to opus-build after 2 Sonnet attempts.
reviewNotes: Clean targeted fix. 2 files, 5 insertions, 4 deletions. (1) Double assertion (as unknown as StudentLink[]) for relationship_type string→union mismatch — pragmatic workaround for Supabase type inference. (2) Replaced !inner join with regular join + .not() filter — avoids SelectQueryError while preserving filtering. (3) Added type assertions for homework array and StudentData object. Build passes with 0 TS errors. MS-004 guardian portal is now unblocked.

task: Parent/Guardian Portal for Student Progress Tracking (RETRY)
description: FIX BUILD: Add guardian_students and guardian_invites table types to database.types.ts per spec Section 1. Also fix: (1) copy button visual feedback on success, (2) replace 'any' types with proper interfaces, (3) render AI tutor conversations inline (read-only) instead of linking. Do NOT re-implement — existing code in commit 0cb7ddb is high quality.
reviewNotes: Significant progress: TS errors reduced from 26 to 2. guardian_students and guardian_invites types correctly added (103 lines, proper Row/Insert/Update/Relationships). All 4 minor fixes done well: copy button feedback with 'Copied!' state, proper interfaces replacing any types, inline expandable conversation viewer, null guard. Remaining 2 TS errors: (1) guardian/dashboard/page.tsx:142 — StudentLink.relationship_type expects union 'parent'|'guardian'|'sibling'|'other' but DB Row type returns string — needs type assertion or interface relaxation; (2) guardian/students/[id]/page.tsx:177 — homework_assignments!inner join resolves to SelectQueryError despite FK relationship existing in types — likely Supabase postgrest-js type inference edge case. Build still fails. Escalated to Opus build after 2 Sonnet attempts.

description: Build complete teacher-facing homework system: creation interface with multiple question types, grading queue, submission review, and student feedback. This completes the core learning loop (lessons → quizzes → homework → teacher feedback).
reviewNotes: Solid implementation. 30 files, 5,699 insertions. Full homework system: QuestionBuilder (5 question types incl. true/false), GradingInterface with keyboard shortcuts (0-9 scoring, N/P nav), QuestionRenderer, HomeworkList for both teacher and student views. 6 API routes with proper auth/role checks and Zod validation. DB migration with RLS policies, triggers, enum extension, and helper functions. Build passes cleanly. Bilingual AR/EN throughout. Student homework page refactored to use shared StudentHomeworkList component (~160 lines removed). Issues: (1) N+1 query pattern in GET /api/homework and /submissions — 4 separate queries per assignment for stats enrichment, will be slow at scale, (2) Quick feedback buttons in GradingInterface English-only despite bilingual design elsewhere, (3) response_file_urls is JSONB in migration but string[] in TS — works but could be tighter. None are blockers at current scale.

task: Build diagnostic assessment system for student placement
description: Create adaptive diagnostic assessment that tests students across grade levels to determine appropriate starting point. Includes question pool, adaptive logic, placement results, and dashboard integration.
reviewNotes: Diagnostic assessment system fully implemented with database schema, API routes, adaptive logic, and UI components. All acceptance criteria met.

task: Commit WIP tutor improvements and validate OpenAI Responses API migration
description: Validate and commit 2 uncommitted files: route.ts (Vercel AI SDK → OpenAI Responses API migration, 657 lines changed) and page.tsx (delete conversations + hide internal tools, 114 lines changed). Fix any issues found during validation.
reviewNotes: Solid implementation after retry. Three commits: (1) backend refactor (Vercel AI SDK → OpenAI Responses API, manual tool loop, UIMessage stream construction), (2) frontend improvements (delete conversations + hidden tools), (3) TS fix (type casts for msg.role and response, empty string for null content). Build passes with zero errors. All spec criteria met. Frontend delete + hidden tools correct. createErrorStream helper present. Commit messages follow conventional format. Working tree clean except ops/.
